analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition

bottleneck.description: Every GEMM in the model executes on the CUDA-core SIMT path, not the Tensor Cores. All eight aten::mm kernels (and the addmm prologue GEMM) are dispatched to cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x128 / _128x256 kernels. The defining evidence is smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 on every GEMM and aggregated.tensor_core_active_pct = 0.0 across all nine GEMM operators. The cause is the fp32 input dtype: cuBLAS/cutlass route fp32 matmuls to the scalar SIMT FFMA path because the FP32 Tensor-Core (TF32/HMMA) path is not engaged for plain float32 unless TF32 is explicitly allowed or inputs are reduced-precision. The result is that a Tensor-Core-class Blackwell GPU runs these matmuls on its slowest GEMM datapath, leaving the dominant compute engine completely idle.

transformation.description: Insert dtype casts so the matmul inputs are a Tensor-Core-eligible reduced precision (bf16 preferred for numerical range on inference, fp16 acceptable) at the aten::mm / aten::addmm boundary, casting the output back to fp32 for the activation epilogue. This switches cutlass/cuBLAS kernel selection from the cutlass_80_simt_sgemm SIMT path to a *_tensorop_* HMMA path, lighting up smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active. If full bf16 is undesirable, the minimal variant is to enable TF32 (torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True / torch._inductor.config.use_mixed_mm and conv/matmul tf32), which alone moves fp32 mm onto the TF32 Tensor-Core datapath. As a graph pass: walk for aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default nodes, insert aten.to.dtype (or prims.convert_element_type) on the matmul operands, and insert a converting node on the result before its activation consumer. This pass is the root of the dependency DAG: it must run before any buffer-registering pass because weight buffers are allocated at the runtime dtype.
transformation.location: Inductor post-grad FX graph (Aten IR), applied to all aten.mm.default and aten.addmm.default nodes in MLPActivations.forward
fx_steps: if node.op != 'call_function' or node.target not in matmul_ops:
# cast the two matmul tensor operands (a, b) to TARGET_DTYPE
mat_args = node.args[1:] if node.target is torch.ops.aten.addmm.default else node.args
base = 1 if node.target is torch.ops.aten.addmm.default else 0
# addmm bias also cast to keep cutlass epilogue homogeneous
if node.target is torch.ops.aten.addmm.default and isinstance(node.args[0], Node):
# cast result back to fp32 for downstream activation
transformation.code_hint: Minimal alternative requiring no FX surgery: torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True; torch.set_float32_matmul_precision('high'). Validate accuracy delta on the model output before committing to bf16.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Weight + activation matmul operands halve in bytes under bf16/fp16 (fp32->16-bit), roughly halving DRAM read volume for the GEMM operands; TF32-only variant keeps fp32 storage so no memory traffic change, gain is purely Tensor-Core compute.

optimizations.notes: Mechanism is unambiguous: smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 on 100% of GEMM kernels on a Tensor-Core GPU. Confidence high on the diagnosis and on Tensor-Core engagement; impact magnitude is a relative estimate only because duration_ns is ncu-inflated 2-5x. Conservative 50% reduction applied to the de-duplicated GEMM total of 879,555 ns (the eight distinct aten::mm/addmm entries, NOT the double-counted prologue). Validate bf16 numerics; tanh/gelu epilogues are computed in fp32 after the cast-back so activation accuracy is unaffected.

bottleneck.description: The skinny output GEMMs (2048->512, op_id=9 and op_id=19) and the small input GEMMs (512->2048, op_id=3 and op_id=13) are severely wave- and launch-starved. The 2048->512 kernels use cutlass_80_simt_sgemm_128x128 with grid_dim [8,1,22] = 176 blocks; against the device SM count this is well under one full wave, so most SMs sit idle. The counters confirm it: sm__throughput = 6.3%, achieved_occupancy = 8.3%, eligible_cycles_pct = 12.0% (< 20 -> latency/launch-bound on Blackwell where warp_cycles_per_instruction is unavailable), and dram__throughput = 2.85%. The fixed cutlass_80 tile is the wrong shape for these dimensions and there is no autotuning selecting a better tile.

transformation.description: Enable Inductor GEMM autotuning so the compiler benchmarks Triton matmul templates and cutlass/cuBLAS candidates and selects per-shape-optimal tiles instead of the single default cutlass_80_simt tile. With OPT-1 applied, the candidate set includes *_tensorop_* templates; autotune will pick a tile that fills more waves for the [256,512] and [256,2048] outputs, raising occupancy above the current 8-16%. This is configuration-level (no node surgery) but it directly changes the kernel chosen for each aten.mm.default / aten.addmm.default node. Must run after OPT-1 because the eligible template set (Tensor-Core vs SIMT) depends on operand dtype.
transformation.location: Inductor compile configuration for the MLPActivations graph (affects all aten.mm.default / aten.addmm.default lowerings)
transformation.code_hint: Equivalent one-liner at call site: torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune'). First compile is slower (benchmarks tiles); cache persists for replay.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Negligible direct memory change; gain is occupancy/wave-fill on the wave-starved 2048->512 and 512->2048 GEMMs (sm_throughput 6.3% -> expected >30% with a fitted tile).

optimizations.notes: Impact estimate is relative and conservative; applied to the four under-occupied GEMMs (273,249 ns of cutlass_128x128 + the two 512->2048 entries). Capped at medium confidence because no MCP search / fx-patterns.md was available to confirm the exact tile autotune picks; the wave-starvation diagnosis itself (176 blocks, 8.3% occupancy, 6.3% sm_throughput) is direct from counters.

bottleneck.description: Each layer currently runs as two kernels: a cutlass GEMM that writes the full [256,2048] (or [256,512]) result to DRAM, immediately followed by a separate triton_poi_fused_addmm_* kernel that reloads that result, adds bias, applies the activation, and writes it back. The GEMM and its bias+activation epilogue are not fused into a single kernel. Evidence: the cutlass GEMM kernels (e.g. k_00041) and the triton_poi_fused_addmm_gelu_1 epilogue (k_00042) are distinct launches on the same stream, back to back. The epilogue triton kernels are memory-bound (dram__throughput 27-33% for relu/gelu/silu, achieved_occupancy 38% aggregate) and exist only to consume the GEMM output -> a redundant DRAM round-trip of the activation tensor plus an extra launch per layer.

transformation.description: Once a Triton GEMM template is active (OPT-2), fold the bias add and the pointwise activation into the GEMM epilogue so each layer is a single kernel. At the Aten IR level this is the standard addmm-epilogue fusion: the aten.addmm.default node (bias + mm) plus its consuming aten.relu/gelu/silu/tanh pointwise node collapse into one Triton matmul-with-epilogue invocation, eliminating one kernel launch and one full read+write of the layer activation per layer (four layers). Inductor performs this automatically when the matmul is lowered to a Triton template rather than an extern cutlass kernel, which is why this depends on OPT-2 selecting the Triton template.
transformation.location: Inductor post-grad fusion over aten.addmm.default + {aten.relu, aten.gelu, aten.silu, aten.tanh}.default consumer pairs
fx_steps: # Enabled implicitly by OPT-2 Triton GEMM templates + epilogue fusion config:
ind.max_autotune_gemm_backends = 'TRITON'  # extern cutlass kernels cannot fuse a Triton epilogue
# No manual node surgery: Inductor's pointwise scheduler fuses the activation
# (aten.gelu.default etc.) into the Triton matmul template epilogue once the
# GEMM is a Triton template node rather than an ExternKernel (cutlass) node.
transformation.code_hint: Verify fusion landed by inspecting the generated Triton: the per-layer triton_poi_fused_addmm_* standalone kernels should disappear, replaced by a single triton_mm_* kernel with the activation inlined in the epilogue.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full DRAM write + read of each layer's activation tensor (4 layers; the largest is [256,2048] fp32 = 2 MB written then reread). Saves ~4 kernel launches per forward pass.

optimizations.notes: Smallest absolute win (the standalone epilogue kernels are only 0.64% of attributed time) but it is a clean structural improvement that also removes launch overhead masked by ncu inflation. Depends on OPT-2 because extern cutlass GEMM kernels cannot host a fused Triton epilogue; only the Triton matmul template can. Confidence capped at medium (no fx-patterns.md citation available to confirm Inductor fuses all four activation variants into the template epilogue on this torch 2.11 build).

global_notes: 1 operator below 1% threshold omitted (combined 0.64% of attributed time): aten::addmm activation-epilogue aggregate (14,912 ns).

EDGE CASE fused_kernel_double_count: operator 'layer::unique::prologue' (is_fused=true) is an NVTX layer-deduplication range whose 24 kernels are the SAME cutlass/triton kernels also attributed individually to the aten::mm and aten::addmm operators. It accounts for 57.56% of the naive time-budget sum and represents ~3 bundled iterations of the 8-kernel forward pattern. All optimization sizing was therefore computed against the de-duplicated per-operator GEMM total (879,555 ns across the eight distinct aten::mm/addmm entries), NOT against the inflated 2,343,080 ns sum.

warp_cycles_per_instruction is null on every operator: expected on Blackwell (RTX PRO 6000), where ncu removed that counter. Latency-bound diagnosis therefore uses eligible_cycles_pct < 20 as the indicator (op_id=9/19 at 12.0% qualify).

ASSUMPTION: no knowledge/hardware-limits.md was present in the repo, so the RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell SM count and FP32 ridge point were taken from general Blackwell (GB202-class) knowledge rather than a project table. Waves/SM was reasoned qualitatively (176 blocks << one wave) rather than with an exact sm_count divisor. Device_name was non-null so the A100 fallback was not used.

ASSUMPTION: no MCP tools (context7 / exa-search / memory / sequential-thinking) were available and knowledge/fx-patterns.md was absent. FX surgery was written from known torch.fx + torch._inductor APIs; per fallback rules all novel transforms (OPT-2, OPT-3) are capped at medium confidence. The dtype/Tensor-Core diagnosis (OPT-1) remains high confidence because it rests entirely on direct hardware counters (smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0, tensor_core_active_pct = 0).

Dependency DAG (no cycles): OPT-1 (dtype promotion, root) -> OPT-2 (GEMM autotune; eligible template set depends on operand dtype) -> OPT-3 (epilogue fusion; requires a Triton GEMM template, not the extern cutlass kernel). Apply strictly in this order.
duration_ns values are ncu counter-collection inflated (2-5x). All relative_duration_reduction_ns figures are intra-profile relative estimates, not wall-clock latency predictions.

No multi_stream_overlap (all kernels stream_id=7), no cuda_graph_replay (compile_mode=inductor), no dynamic_shapes (per-kernel duration CV < 5%), no high_unattributed (unattributed_kernels empty; 14 pre-NVTX init kernels correctly excluded), no clock_domain anomaly.
